items.name: PhosFate
items.description: PhosBind is a machine learning framework that leverages ESM2 protein embeddings to predict and classify anion-binding sites, with a focus on phosphate recognition. It enables data-driven insights into protein–anion interactions for applications in biomolecular design and nutrient recovery.

items.name: Cilia-motion-phenotyping-
items.description: Fully automated and interpretable framework for motion phenotype classification from high-speed microscopy videos. Provides reproducible feature extraction and hierarchical machine learning tools for biomedical video analysis.

items.description: Explainable AI in Healthcare: Cancer Dataset is a machine learning framework that uses interpretable algorithms (like SHAP or LIME) on oncology data to provide clear, human-understandable clinical justifications alongside its cancer diagnoses and risk predictions.

items.name: federated-learning-differential-privacy

items.name: Machine-Learning-Model-for-Prediction-of-The-P-E-loop
items.description: A hybrid modelling framework was developed for the prediction of the evolving hysteresis P-E loop of NBT. • Hybrid framework integrating Mathematical, DNN, and KNN models shows excellent loop prediction with an average R² of 0.981. • The hybrid model accurately captured loop parameters Pᵣ, Ec , <A> and hysteresis loop scaling behaviour.
